@documentation.@comment: Universal base for every domain document in the graph. Carries audit timestamps, required provenance (birth certificate), optional derivation ancestry (derived_from), optional soft-delete tombstone (archived_at), context tagging, and external identity. Registry meta-classes (SchemaModule, SchemaMigration) are deliberately outside this grammar.

provenance.@comment: Birth certificate — exactly one per document, describing origination. Updates are attributed via the commit graph (the biography), deliberately not on the document.

derived_from.@comment: N-ary derivation relation: this document was derived or synthesized from these sources.

archived_at.@comment: Soft-delete tombstone. Reference-preserving retirement; generic tools filter archived entities uniformly; physical deletion remains exceptional.

@documentation.@comment: Role marker: things other things can be derived from (notes, audio, routines, entities that beget).
Role marker: things other things can be tagged/classified with (locations, projects, areas).

Pure role marker: things with a canonical temporal instant. Any concrete class implementing Anchored MUST declare class-level @metadata.anchor_field naming one of its own xsd:dateTime fields; that field holds the canonical temporal instant. If the field's value is absent on a document, temporally-relative triggers referencing it are dormant and evaluators MUST skip them explicitly.

Birth certificate — exactly one per Entity, describing origination. Updates are attributed via the commit graph (the biography), deliberately not on the document. agent follows a naming grammar: service:<name> (e.g. service:ingestd), user:<name> (e.g. user:basti), ext:<name> (e.g. ext:mail) — reserved so a future first-class Agent migration can parse historical strings.
Minimal blessed context for frictionless tagging (e.g. #learning). Shared kernel concept so cross-extension tag queries interoperate.
Link to the same entity in an external system.
Registry record for an installed schema module, keyed by name. Tracks origin and version for the composer. Plugin requirement checks validate required classes against exports.
Registry record for an applied schema migration, keyed by module and filename. Tracks which migrations have been applied to a database.
Immutable record of a single state transition on a document. Written atomically with the status change by the Repository layer; never updated or deleted.
